visual_features.overall_style: Bright, family‑friendly, and slightly promotional. A mix of modern web layout (full‑width hero, horizontal nav) with vibrant, poster‑like event graphics. Not minimalist; intentionally colorful and energetic to appeal to families and kids while keeping core UI (nav, body text) relatively clean.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: 1) Brand/logo area top‑left establishes identity. 2) Horizontal primary navigation spans the width and is highly visible with uppercase labels and clear separation. 3) Below, a full‑width hero strip of four colorful promotional panels dominates attention via strong imagery and saturated colors. 4) A large typographic introduction section below in dark aqua/blue on white guides the eye into core content. The active nav item (HOME) is highlighted in blue for hierarchy. The floating blue chat button at bottom‑left stands out as a secondary focal action.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Moderate to generous whitespace in structural UI (header, nav, intro copy). The nav has clear spacing between items; the intro text block is surrounded by comfortable lateral margins and vertical padding. The hero promotional flyers are visually dense but contained in their own band, while the overall page margins and separation between header, hero, and body text prevent crowding.
unique_elements: Highly colorful, poster‑style hero section comprising multiple adjacent promotional flyers/cards in a horizontal strip.
Scripted, illustrative logo with aquatic icon and multi‑color wordmark distinct from the otherwise simple sans‑serif UI typography.
Floating circular chat/help button anchored at the lower left of the viewport.
Thin blue top bar with phone number and 'Staff Log In' link as a light-weight utility header.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Implicit 12‑column desktop grid. The hero section shows four equal-width columns (each effectively spanning 3 of 12 columns). Content is centered within a max‑width container while some elements (hero images) extend nearly full width.
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Classic top‑anchored layout: utility bar + logo/nav header, followed by a full‑width hero band, then a centered content column. Horizontal nav, full‑width hero, and linear vertical scroll.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: From top to bottom: (1) Slim blue utility bar containing contact number and staff login link, right-aligned. (2) Main header with logo on the left and horizontal nav aligned centrally/right, on a white background with clear separation. (3) Hero strip directly under nav: a row of four equal-width promotional panels. (4) Introductory text section on white with large, readable paragraph typography. Subsequent sections (not fully visible) likely continue in vertical blocks.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Left alignment for logo and body copy; right alignment for phone number and utility links. Nav items are horizontally centered within the header region and aligned on a baseline. Hero cards align on a single row with equal widths. Text within promotional panels varies by design but is confined to each card. The main intro block appears left-aligned within a centered content container.
responsive_hints: Full‑width hero cards suggest a carousel or responsive slider that could stack or scroll horizontally on smaller screens.
Horizontal nav likely collapses into a hamburger or stacked menu for mobile; uppercase labels and spacing indicate tap‑target awareness.
Floating chat button is positioned relative to the viewport, implying responsive repositioning (e.g., offset from bottom/left on smaller screens).
Content is placed within a max‑width container with margins on larger screens, indicating fluid layout that scales with viewport width.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top‑to‑bottom flow with distinct full‑width bands: a narrow utility bar, then a header band with logo and nav, followed by a full‑width hero band featuring a four‑column card strip, then a large full‑width but center‑constrained text block. The hero band visually behaves as a single section containing multiple promotional cards laid out horizontally. Below that, the main narrative content starts with large introductory text.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: The header (utility bar + main nav) occupies a relatively shallow vertical area (~10–15% of initial viewport height), leaving most of the above‑the‑fold space to the hero promotion band (~40–50% of the initial viewport). The intro text section fills the remaining visible height with wide margins and generous line height, emphasizing readability over density. Within the header, the logo consumes the left portion while the nav uses most of the horizontal width; vertical padding keeps it airy. The floating chat button consumes minimal space but is visually prominent due to color and isolation.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Overall low to medium density. Structural UI is spacious and readable with ample white background. The hero promotional cards themselves are visually dense with graphics and text (more like printed posters), but they are isolated in a single band so the page does not feel cluttered. The intro section uses large text and relaxed line-height, contributing to a more editorial and welcoming feel.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye starts at the top-left logo, moves right across the nav (classic F-pattern), then drops down to the colorful hero band. Within the hero, the eye likely scans left-to-right across the four promotional cards. From there, the user’s gaze is drawn down to the large intro paragraph centered in the next section, then continues vertically through ensuing content. The floating chat button at the bottom-left acts as a peripheral but noticeable action point that the eye catches once main content has been scanned.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The only clearly visible interactive button pattern is the floating chat/help button: a circular button (~48–56px diameter) with a solid bright blue (#0084FF) fill, a white chat bubble icon centered inside, and likely a subtle drop shadow or elevation to distinguish it from the background. Edges are fully rounded (50% border radius). Text-based nav items and links elsewhere are styled as plain text links without obvious button backgrounds in this screenshot.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: For primary actions and floating chat: a <button> or <a> element positioned fixed at the viewport bottom-left, containing an <svg> or <i> icon for the chat bubble. For text CTAs in content, likely <a> elements styled as links. If primary CTAs exist elsewhere on the site, they would sensibly follow the same color system (solid aqua/blue background, white text) and be implemented as <button> or <a class="btn btn-primary">.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The hero section comprises four card-like promotional panels. Each panel occupies roughly 25% of the width, with no visible gaps between cards (edge-to-edge). Each card has its own internal graphic design, including photographic backgrounds, vector art, and text. Card edges appear straight (0px radius) and share a common baseline. The cards rely on strong internal imagery rather than shadows; separation is defined by their differing artwork rather than borders or shadows. Vertical height is uniform across the row.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Each card would likely be a container
within a flexbox or grid parent (
) that distributes four children evenly. Inside, the structure might be or background-image with overlaid text, or nested
blocks for title, subtitle, and CTA. In this screenshot, the text appears baked into images, but a semantic implementation would use headings (

), paragraphs, and links inside the card container.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit form fields are visible in the screenshot. Based on the overall style, form elements would likely be clean, border-based inputs on white background with subtle rounding (2–4px radius), dark gray text, and aqua/blue highlights on focus. Labels would likely be placed above inputs for clarity, with moderate vertical spacing.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected pattern: stacked vertical forms with labels above each <input> or <select>, full-width fields inside a form container (<form>). Related fields grouped using <fieldset> and <legend> or section headings. Validation messaging likely appears below inputs in small-sized text, with color-coded semantic styling.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Main navigation is a horizontal menu on a white background. Items are uppercase sans-serif text, evenly spaced, likely around 14–16px, with a dark gray or black base color. The active item ('HOME') is colored in bright aqua/blue (#00A7E5) and may have an underline or subtle bottom border (not clearly visible but implied by spacing). There is a thin line separating the nav from the hero section, giving a clear boundary. The utility bar above uses smaller text with a blue background and white links.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Standard semantic markup: <nav> containing an unordered list (<ul>) of list items (<li>) each with an <a>. The active item has a class like .active applied. The utility bar is likely a separate <div class="top-bar"> containing inline elements or a flex container (<span> for phone, <a> for staff login). On mobile, this pattern would typically collapse into a toggle/hamburger menu using JavaScript and CSS.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No traditional text lists (like bullet lists) are clearly visible in the main area, but the nav itself is visually a horizontal list. It is low density, with ample spacing between items and no visible vertical separators; hover states would presumably change color and/or add an underline. Promotional copy inside hero cards uses bullet-like stacking of lines but appears embedded in images.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Primary list structure is expected to be <ul> with flexbox to display nav items horizontally. Any feature lists elsewhere on the site would likely use standard <ul>/<li> with custom bullets (e.g., colored dots or icons) aligned left, implemented using flexbox media objects when paired with icons or thumbnails.

heading.visual_style: Headings and nav items appear as a straightforward sans-serif with clean strokes and relatively neutral geometry—neither overly rounded nor condensed. The tone is professional but approachable, matching a community facility context.

body.visual_style: Body text looks like a clean humanist or neo‑grotesque sans-serif, with good legibility and moderate x-height. The intro paragraph uses larger sizing and relaxed line height for readability.

font_families.code: "Source Code Pro", Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, "Courier New", monospace

spacing_system.component_padding: Typical interactive components (buttons, nav items, cards) would use 8–16px horizontal and vertical padding (e.g., nav item clickable area ~8px vertical padding; hero card internal padding if text-based ~16–24px).
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections (header/hero, hero/content, etc.) is roughly 32–48px, providing clear separation and visual breathing room.

interaction_hints: Nav items likely change text color to primary aqua/blue and/or show an underline or bottom border on hover; active state persists with primary color.
Floating chat button likely uses a hover effect such as a slight scale-up and/or shadow intensification, with a pointer cursor; on click, it probably opens a chat panel or contact dialog.
Hero promotional cards may be clickable; hover feedback could include a subtle zoom of the image, slight shadow, or a border highlight to indicate interactivity.
Links within body text probably shift color to a brighter blue and may be underlined on hover for accessibility.

Transitions/animations are likely subtle and utility-driven: 150–250ms ease-in-out transitions on color, shadow, and transform for buttons and interactive tiles; any hero carousel behavior would animate slides horizontally.
